title: Track Memo Processing State Across the Pipeline
creator: [You]

userStory.persona: a developer building the Utterd pipeline
userStory.want: a persistent record of each memo's processing status
userStory.soThat: memos are never processed twice, failed memos can be retried, and no memo is silently lost

context: State is stored in a local SQLite database. The schema supports the statuses needed for Epic 1: detected, transcribing, transcribed, failed. Later epics will extend this schema with additional columns for classification and note-writing status.

acceptanceCriteria.given: the watcher has emitted a new file path
acceptanceCriteria.when: the state tracker receives that file path for the first time
acceptanceCriteria.then: a new record is created with status detected, the file path, a SHA-256 content hash, the current timestamp, and a retry count of 0

acceptanceCriteria.given: a memo record already exists with a matching file path and content hash
acceptanceCriteria.when: the state tracker receives the same file path again
acceptanceCriteria.then: no duplicate record is created and the existing record is unchanged

acceptanceCriteria.given: a memo has been previously processed and deleted from Voice Memos, then re-recorded with different content
acceptanceCriteria.when: the new file appears at the same path but with different audio content
acceptanceCriteria.then: the different content hash causes it to be treated as a new memo with its own record
the status updates to transcribed and the record stores the transcription text, confidence score, audio duration in seconds, and a transcription timestamp
the status updates to failed, the error message and failure stage are stored, and the retry count increments by 1

acceptanceCriteria.given: the daemon was terminated while a memo was in transcribing status
acceptanceCriteria.then: that memo is identifiable by querying for records in detected or transcribing status so the orchestrator can requeue them

acceptanceCriteria.given: the SQLite database file does not exist
acceptanceCriteria.when: the daemon starts for the first time
acceptanceCriteria.then: the database and schema are created automatically at the defined local path

acceptanceCriteria.given: the SQLite database already exists from a previous session
acceptanceCriteria.then: the existing data is preserved and accessible

outOfScope: Classification or note-writing columns — those are added when Epics 2 and 3 begin. No UI for viewing state — that's Epic 4. The state tracker does not decide what to do with failed memos — retry policy belongs to the orchestrator in Story 5.
